clues.clue: This armored hero is genius industrialist Tony Stark.
This web-slinging hero is photographer Peter Parker.
This star-spangled hero, frozen for decades, is the former scrawny recruit Steve Rogers.
This Sorcerer Supreme is former neurosurgeon Stephen Strange.
This Norse god of thunder first appeared in 'Journey into Mystery' #83 in 1962.
The Kellogg brothers developed corn flakes in this Michigan 'Cereal City.'
This 'General' company, a Kellogg's rival, makes Cheerios and Lucky Charms.
Despite its name, this Post cereal of crunchy nuggets contains neither grapes nor nuts.
Breakfast cereal was first promoted as health food at a sanitarium run by this Kellogg brother, a physician.
James Caleb Jackson's 1863 'Granula' is considered the first manufactured one of these.
In this real-estate game, players buy properties such as Boardwalk and Park Place.
In this game, players score points forming words from lettered tiles.
In this whodunit, players deduce the culprit, the weapon and the room.
This ancient Asian strategy game of territory uses black and white stones on a gridded board.
Boards for this ancient Egyptian game were found in Tutankhamun's tomb.
Henry Ford's assembly line mass-produced this affordable early 'Model.'
Karl Benz patented an early gasoline-powered version of this vehicle in 1886.
This Scottish engineer's improved steam engine inspired a unit of power named for him.
The Wright brothers achieved the first powered flight in 1903 at this North Carolina site.
This British-French supersonic airliner, retired in 2003, cruised at twice the speed of sound.
This cannibal psychiatrist assists the FBI from behind bars.
This burned, bladed-glove killer stalks teenagers in their dreams on Elm Street.
This hockey-masked killer haunts Camp Crystal Lake.
This 'Psycho' motel owner is disturbingly devoted to his late mother.
In 'No Country for Old Men,' this hitman kills using a captive-bolt cattle gun.

final.clue: These five interlocking colored loops represent the union of the world's inhabited continents.
